fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Pennsylvania Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity pennsylvania, how to transfer audiologist license to pennsylvania, audiologist compact pennsylvania, pennsylvania audiologist endorsement application

board.name: State Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Pennsylvania Department of State, Bureau of Professional and Occupational Affairs
board.mailing_address:
Department of State
P.O. Box 2649
Harrisburg, PA 17105-2649

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series administered by the Educational Testing Service
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 20 hours of continuing education per renewal cycle

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Pennsylvania.

steps.title: Meet Educational Requirements
steps.description: Complete a master's or doctoral degree in audiology from an approved educational institution, which must include a supervised clinical practicum of at least 375 clock hours.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Successfully complete a minimum of 9 months of full-time (or 18 months of part-time) supervised professional experience (SPE) or clinical fellowship.

steps.title: Pass National Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Series Examination in Audiology (ETS Exam #5342).

steps.title: Register and Apply via PALS
steps.description: Create an account on the PALS (Pennsylvania Licensing System) website, then complete and submit the online application for an Audiologist license.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload all required supporting documents, including official transcripts, verification of supervised experience, and other necessary forms, directly through your PALS account.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal History Record Check
steps.description: Complete the required Criminal History Record Check (CHRC) as part of the application process through the PALS system.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Pennsylvania audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Pennsylvania
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Pennsylvania, navigate to the PALS (Pennsylvania Licensing System) portal and register for a new account to begin your application.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the PALS (Pennsylvania Licensing System) homepage.
2. Click 'New User Registration' or 'Register' to create a new PALS account.
3. Follow the prompts to complete your registration, then log in and select 'New Application' to choose 'Audiologist' from the list of professions.
